content.rule: Input forms MUST always include a visible label.
Initial state can use placeholder as label.
Once content entered, label moves above.
Error validation should happen at the latest when the user clicks out of the text entry focus.
Provides a brief example of the expected input content. It should be used only to model good input, and not to convey instructions or restrictions for the field.

content.dont: Simply repeat the field label or description in the placeholder.

content.rule: Use placeholder text when: An example would model the correct input; Naming conventions or restrictions should be reinforced; The field label could be interpreted in different ways, and an example would reduce that ambiguity.

content.dont: Use placeholder text if the label text is sufficient.

content.rule: Keep placeholders concise and brief: No full sentences. No ending punctuation. Between 2-5 words. The placeholder should scan instantly and disappear without cognitive load.

content.dont: Include restrictions and rules such as character limits, allowed or restricted characters, or formatting rules in placeholder text.

content.rule: Placeholder text must never replace a field's label.
All critical guidance must remain visible after the user starts typing.
The UI must remain usable if placeholder text is removed.

contexts: Screenshots showing text input in product forms (2026-02-27)
Screenshots showing various text input states in real UI
Captures from 2025-03-26 and 2025-07-22 showing historical usage
